title: Render "ghosting" / flickering of pointer when using --info=inline [or --info=inline:]
author: DANIII3L
body: ### Checklist - [x] I have read through the manual page (`man fzf`) - [x] I have searched through the existing issues - [x] For bug reports, I have checked if the bug is reproducible in the latest version of fzf ### Output of `fzf --version` 0.74.0 (6765f464) ### OS - [x] Linux - [ ] macOS - [ ] Windows - [ ] Etc. ### Shell - [ ] bash - [x] zsh - [ ] fish ### Problem / Steps to reproduce Hi, I'm experiencing a visual rendering issue where the pointer character (󰆧) leaves a "ghost" or overlaps with other text when the --info=inline option is used. This issue does not occur when the option: --info='inline:  ' is not set , as shown here: Example Configuration A minimal example of a configuration that triggers the problem: export FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS="\ --pointer='▸' \ --info='inline'" Expected Behavior The pointer should render cleanly without leaving artifacts. Actual Behavior The pointer image "ghosts" or flickers, leaving behind a visual artifact.

title: nushell: str downcase deprecation warning in completion.nu
author: tsssni
body:

- [x] For bug reports, I have checked if the bug is reproducible in the latest version of fzf

### Output of `fzf --version`

0.73.1

### OS

- [x] Linux
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Windows
- [ ] Etc.

### Shell

- [ ] bash
- [ ] zsh
- [ ] fish

### Problem / Steps to reproduce

```nu
Warning: nu::parser::deprecated

  ⚠ Command deprecated.
     ╭─[/nix/store/vxr7g2i5vcaasw1hr8baxjig55l83kgg-nushell-fzf-integration.nu:288:41]
 287 │       $ssh_configs | append $ssh_configs_d | append $ssh_config_global
 288 │                    | where {|it| ($it | str downcase | str starts-with 'host') or ($it | str downcase | str starts-with 'hostname') }
     ·                                         ──────┬─────
     ·                                               ╰── str downcase was deprecated in 0.114.0 and will be removed in a future release.
 289 │                    | parse --regex '^\s*host(?:name)?\s+(?<hosts>.+)' # Extract hosts after keyword
     ╰────
  help: Use `str lowercase` instead.
```

### Shell

- [ ] bash
- [ ] zsh

### Problem / Steps to reproduce

ctrl-u performs backward-kill-line, but the corresponding ctrl-k for kill-line is not bound by default. It _is_ documented in the man page, but if there are no particular reasons not to, it would be nice to have this as a default keybind.

title: Preview should scroll through wrapped lines instead of actual ones
author: nylvim
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have read through the manual page (`man fzf`)
- [x] I have searched through the existing issues
- [x] For bug reports, I have checked if the bug is reproducible in the latest version of fzf

### Output of `fzf --version`

0.73.1 (ce4bef75)

### OS

- [x] Linux
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Windows
- [ ] Etc.

### Shell

- [ ] bash
- [x] zsh
- [ ] fish

### Problem / Steps to reproduce

With the `wrap` style enabled for the preview, if you try to scroll over a very long line which overflows the screen, you jump directly to the next actual line, skipping remaining text. Long jumps like this seem annoying when previewing text, even if they only happen within one screen.

I think it'll be more intuitive if the preview can scroll one wrapped line each time. This could be an option but more preferably by default.

title: Use tmux 3.7 floating panes for `--popup`
author: junegunn
body:
Tmux 3.7 introduced "floating panes"

- https://github.com/tmux/tmux/issues/5135

From my understanding, unlike popups, floating panes allow multiple floating panes to be open simultaneously, support switching between panes, and can be resized with the mouse.

Let's investigate whether we can make --popup use floating panes instead of popups.

title: Create a clear AI Policy
author: norwd
body:
Hello @junegunn,

I'd like to talk about your AI policy, or rather, the current lack of one.

In general, GitHub recommends [adding a CONTRIBUTING.md file], so even if fzf doesn't adopt an anti-AI policy, I think that for clarity the position of the fzf project on the use of AI, along with guidelines on disclosure etc, should be explicitly put in a CONTRIBUTING.md file.

At time of writing, I've only been able to [detect][detection of ai usage] one usage of AI in the whole of fzf's history, that being  [commit `290b18d`], which was a [code review suggestion made by copilot]. This suggests that the existing community of contributors and users of fzf are already either anti AI, or don't use it without necessarily being against its use. However, the lack of a clear rules leaves the door open to a vibe coded PR being introduced tomorrow that introduces a major security bug, or deletes the entire contents of users' home directories.

[adding a CONTRIBUTING.md file]: https://docs.github.com/en/communities/setting-up-your-project-for-healthy-contributions/setting-guidelines-for-repository-contributors#adding-a-contributingmd-file
[commit `290b18d`]: https://github.com/junegunn/fzf/commit/290b18d9fe1f63f5ed7ab5639bad034563991146
[code review suggestion made by copilot]: https://github.com/junegunn/fzf/pull/4630#discussion_r3200115450
[detection of ai usage]: https://codeberg.org/norwd-forks/mirror-automation/actions/runs/28/jobs/6/attempt/1

Below are some reasons why using AI can result in poorer code quality and subsequent security incidences:

## Poor Code Quality

Vibe coding / agentic workflows result in poorer code quality, and relaxed oversight practices. These effects may be compounded by the common practice of using additional LLM-based tooling to provide code-reviews.

* [How I Dropped Our Production Database and Now Pay 10% More for AWS](http://archive.today/2026.03.06-144058/https://alexeyondata.substack.com/p/how-i-dropped-our-production-database)
* [Claude Tested Everything Except the One Thing That Mattered (AI agent refuses to follow explicit instructions to test `createPost()` in increasingly erratic ways)](http://archive.today/2026.03.09-201135/https://christophermeiklejohn.com/ai/claude/2026/03/08/claude-tested-everything-except-the-one-thing-that-mattered.html)
* [Amazon calls engineers for a “deep dive” internal meeting to discuss “GenAI”-related outages](https://ghostarchive.org/archive/3TfgF)
* GitClear has released reports in [2024](https://www.gitclear.com/coding_on_copilot_data_shows_ais_downward_pressure_on_code_quality) and [2025](https://www.gitclear.com/ai_assistant_code_quality_2025_research) indicating a worsening of key code quality metrics correlating with increased LLM adoption.

### Deskilling

There is increasing evidence to show that LLMs negatively impact developers' coding abilities:

* [Brains show less activity when completing tasks with LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.08872) compared to completing tasks with search or completing tasks without digital help.
* [Developers who use early-2025 LLMs](https://metr.org/blog/2025-07-10-early-2025-ai-experienced-os-dev-study/) reported higher subjective performance, but were measured to have lower objective performance. This gap between subjective and objective performance was considered notable.
* In an [Anthropic study](https://www.anthropic.com/research/AI-assistance-coding-skills), learners using LLMs demonstrated lower learning rates on average compared to learners not using LLMs.
* [A recent study uses the term "cognitive surrender"](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=6097646) to describe the way humans tend to offload key critical thinking skills onto LLMs, even when the output is wrong.
* A paper entitled ["AI Assistance Reduces Persistence and Hurts Independent Performance"](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.04721) from April 2026 by academics from MIT, Oxford, UCLA, and Carnegie Mellon showed alarming evidence that performing a variety of tasks with the help of AI for only 10 minutes causes "inpaired unassisted performance and reduced persistence". The researchers noted that "although AI assistance improves performance in the short-term, people perform significantly worse without AI and are more likely to give up"; they also pointed out that "these findings are particularly concerning because persistence is foundational to skill acquisition and is one of the strongest predictors of long-term learning".

### Infosec risks

LLM usage results in massive security holes.

* [Meta Security Researcher's AI Agent Accidentally Deleted Her Emails](http://archive.today/2026.02.26-153034/https://www.pcmag.com/news/meta-security-researchers-openclaw-ai-agent-accidentally-deleted-her-emails)
* [Moltbook's "vibe-coded" breach is the future of security failures](https://thehill.com/opinion/cybersecurity/5744310-ai-powered-security-risks/)
* [In a study evaluating over 500k code samples, LLM-generated code was found to contain more high-risk security vulnerabilities than human-generated code](https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.21634)
* [LLMs make up package names, making them vulnerable to incorporating malicious code in "slopsquatting" attacks](https://www.theregister.com/2025/04/12/ai_code_suggestions_sabotage_supply_chain/) ([Arxiv study](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.10279))

## What You Can Do

I'd like to kindly suggest implementing a strict "No AI" policy. (Or, as I've said, at least clarify the position of the fzf project on AI usage.) This can also help with what seems to be larger and larger influxes of low quality drive-by PRs by AI agents.

You can find other projects that have taken steps to say no to AI here:
https://noai.starlightnet.work/list.html

For a specific policy, I can recommend the following policies for inspiration:

- [wafrn's Contributring Doc](https://codeberg.org/wafrn/wafrn/src/branch/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [Loupe's Use of Generative AI Policy](https://discourse.gnome.org/t/loupe-no-longer-allows-generative-ai-contributions/27327)

Thank you and kind regards :pray:
-- Y. Meyer-Norwood (@norwd)

title: Horizontal mouse wheel events treated as vertical scrolling
author: jason5122
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have read through the manual page (`man fzf`)
- [x] I have searched through the existing issues
- [x] For bug reports, I have checked if the bug is reproducible in the latest version of fzf

### Output of `fzf --version`

0.73.1 (Homebrew)

### OS

- [ ] Linux
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Windows
- [ ] Etc.

### Shell

- [ ] bash
- [x] zsh
- [ ] fish

### Problem / Steps to reproduce

> [!NOTE]
> No AI was used! I noticed you closed contributions due to an influx of low-effort PRs, so I put in the time to keep the code minimal and fully understand the changes. I love your work, please keep going!

One day, I encountered jittering when scrolling up/down on my macOS trackpad in fzf. I realized it was because the horizontal scroll component is factored in, so if you scroll 45° up and right or 45° down and left, the scroll counteracts itself! I propose that horizontal mouse wheel events should *not* count as vertical scrolling.

## Reproducing

1. Use a terminal that supports horizontal scrolling (e.g., Ghostty, Alacritty, Kitty, etc.).
1. Scroll horizontally in `fzf`.
1. Scrolling left should scroll upwards. Scrolling right should scroll downwards.

## The Fix

I believe the issue has to do with [lines 908-915 of light.go](https://github.com/junegunn/fzf/blob/f2e451596c5d23de0069cdc51ea3fec73b7fe835/src/tui/light.go#L908-L915). The logic for SGR mouse wheel events is as follows. Is this intended?

| SGR Mouse Event | Formula     | Scroll    |
| --------------- | ----------- | --------- |
| 64 (up)         | 64 & 1 => 0 | +1 (up)   |
| 65 (down)       | 65 & 1 => 1 | -1 (down) |
| 66 (left)       | 66 & 1 => 0 | +1 (up)   |
| 67 (right)      | 67 & 1 => 1 | -1 (down) |

I [made a branch](https://github.com/junegunn/fzf/compare/master...jason5122:fzf:fix-horizontal-wheel-scroll) with the following changes:

| SGR Mouse Event | Formula     | Scroll      |
| --------------- | ----------- | ----------- |
| 64              | 64 & 3 => 0 | +1          |
| 65              | 65 & 3 => 1 | -1          |
| 66              | 66 & 3 => 2 | 0 (ignored) |
| 67              | 67 & 3 => 3 | 0 (ignored) |

I just made horizontal scrolling a no-op. In the future, we can support proper horizontal scrolling (#4824).

title: Paste command to the command line on enter (using some action) instead of executing it (using `become`)
author: duke1995
body:
I have a bash function that runs `fzf` to select a project from a list of projects:

```
function oc_project_fzf {
    local projects=$(oc get projects)
    echo "$projects" | fzf --tac --no-sort --bind "enter:become(oc project {1})"
}
```

When I run the command, fzf shows the list of projects to choose from, and when I press enter, the command `oc project {1}` is executed using the currently selected project from the list.

However, instead of immediately executing the `oc project {1}` command, I would like fzf to just paste the command to the command line and exit, so that I can see on the command line what command will be executed before actually executing it by pressing enter once more (but this time on the command line, outside of fzf).

Is there a way to achieve this?

### Problem / Steps to reproduce

Similar to #4814, running `seq 1 10 | fzf --tmux` sometimes renders correctly, sometimes creates an empty popup and using `ctrl+C` closes the popup and prints `exit status 2` on the original terminal.

Tmux version is 3.6b, have tried downgrading to 3.6a but issue still persists.

Any ideas on how I could debug further? I have adjusted my affected workflows but the new approach ends up being clunky and would prefer a proper fix.

title: [fish] ␛H, ␍ and ↳ symbols when using fzf-history-widget
author: ritze
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have read through the manual page (`man fzf`)
- [x] I have searched through the existing issues
- [x] For bug reports, I have checked if the bug is reproducible in the latest version of fzf

### Output of `fzf --version`

0.72.0 (6fefe025)

### OS

- [x] Linux
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Windows
- [ ] Etc.

### Shell

- [ ] bash
- [ ] zsh
- [x] fish

### Problem / Steps to reproduce

Not sure if the issues I found are all related to each other or are probably issues for themselves. If you think the issues are not related to each other, I can open further issue tickets for each of them.

I'm running fzf in a fish terminal without any configuration (`fish --no-config`). The behavior is the same with a Ghostty, Alacritty and raw Linux TTY (login without loading any desktop environment) with `/bin/fish` as the user's terminal in `/etc/passwd`.


# ␛H symbols

```fish
$ source /usr/share/fish/vendor_functions.d/fzf_key_bindings.fish
$ echo test
$ fzf-history-widget
```
and when hitting `alt`+`enter` the command prompt will be filled with strange symbols:
```fish
$  ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H    ␛H
```

I had similar issues with different fish scripts using fzf. I figured out that the issue to fix them was to remove the environment `SHELL=/bin/fish` in my Ghosstty config, which then use `SHELL=/bin/bash`, since my desktop configuration is to start my desktop environment using bash, but starting Ghostty with fish as my shell.
I guess the root cause is spawning fish sub-shells instead of bash. With spwaning bash sub-shells it seems to work. But I'm not deep enough into the topic to give a qualified answer to this.


# ␍ symbol and unknown command

Same shell, but starting fzf-history-widget with `--preview`
```fish
$ FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS="--preview" fzf-history-widget
```

The preview window will show `␍` and additionally an issue with the fzf-history-widget function:
```
␍
fish:
--with-nth=2..
^~~~~~~~~~~~~^
```

# Additional "↳" symbols for newline

I modified the fzf-history-widget function to suite to my preferences, but run into same issues (but with without the `--with-nth=2` issue). My modified fzf-history-widget function:

```fish
function _fzf_search_history --description "Search command history"
    set -l -- command_line (commandline)
    set -l -- current_line (commandline -L)
    set -l -- total_lines (count $command_line)
    set -l -- fzf_query (string escape -- $command_line[$current_line])

    set -lx -- FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS \
        --read0 \
        --print0 \
        --multi \
        --bind="focus,resize:bg-transform:if test \\"$FZF_COLUMNS\\" -gt 100 -a \\\\( \\"$FZF_SELECT_COUNT\\" -gt 0 -o \\\\( -z \\"$FZF_WRAP\\" -a (string length -- {}) -gt (math $FZF_COLUMNS - 4) \\\\) -o (string collect -- {2..} | fish_indent | count) -gt 1 \\\\); echo show-preview; else echo hide-preview; end" \
        '--preview "string collect -- (test \\"$FZF_SELECT_COUNT\\" -gt 0; and string collect -- {+2..}) \\"\\n\\" {2..} | fish_indent --ansi"' \
        --bind "ctrl-z:ignore" \
        '--bind=\'ctrl-x:execute-silent(for i in (string split0 -- <{+f}); eval builtin history delete --exact --case-sensitive -- (string escape -n -- $i | string replace -r "^\d+-\d+\\\\\\ \d+:\d+:\d+\\\\\\t" ""); end)+reload(eval $FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND)\'' \
        '--bind "alt-enter:become(string join0 -- (string collect -- {+2..} | fish_indent -i))"' \
        --bind "ctrl-r:toggle-sort" \
        --bind "alt-r:toggle-raw" \
        --preview-window "bottom:4:wrap:follow" \
        --height 30 \
        --min-height "20+" \
        --scheme history \
        --nth=2..,.. \
        --no-multi-line \
        --no-wrap \
        --reverse \
        --freeze-left 2 \
        --highlight-line \
        --accept-nth 2.. \
        --delimiter "\t" \
        --tabstop 2 \
        --ansi \
        --with-shell=(status fish-path)\\ -c \
        (test -r "$FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS_FILE"; and string join -- ' ' <$FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS_FILE) " " \
        $FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS $argv[2..-1]

    set -lx FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS_FILE
    set -lx -- FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND "builtin history -z --show-time='%m-%d %H:%M:%S%t' --color=always"

    # Merge history from other sessions before searching
    test -z "$fish_private_mode"; and builtin history merge

    if set -l result (eval $FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND \| fzf --query=$fzf_query | string split0)
        if test "$total_lines" -eq 1
            commandline -- $result
        else
            set -l a (math $current_line - 1)
            set -l b (math $current_line + 1)
            commandline -- $command_line[1..$a] $result
            commandline -a -- "" $command_line[$b..-1]
        end
    end

    commandline --function repaint
end
```

The preview will add `↳` for each newline even though I didn't customized this behavior. Probably it's related to fish's behavior described in [7015](https://github.com/fish-shell/fish-shell/issues/7015) and [7893](https://github.com/fish-shell/fish-shell/issues/7893).

```fish
␍
↳ ␍FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS="--preview" fzf-history-widget
```

title: Error when wrapping fzf Ctrl+R picker

I'm currently working on adding integration with fzf in my own implementation of a reverse interactive search, and thought I'd try to allow users to use fzf's picker instead if they prefer it, but it spits out a rather opaque error message when I try to test it:

```txt
C:\cygwin64\tmp\fzf-temp-1160247143: line 1: C:\cygwin64\home\Revan\.tools\fzf\bin\fzf.exe: command not found
```

That is the path I installed fzf in, so I'm not sure what I might be doing wrong. [Here is the code I am using to invoke fzf's picker](https://gitlab.com/prodigal.knight/sqlite-history.sh/-/blob/feature/fzf-picker-integration/src/sqlite_i_search.sh#L388)

title: [Suggestion] A suggested footnote for `FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS`
author: GrassGlass
body: ### Checklist - [x] I have read through the manual page (`man fzf`) - [x] I have searched through the existing issues - [x] For bug reports, I have checked if the bug is reproducible in the latest version of fzf ### Output of `fzf --version` 0.73.1 (Fedora) ### OS - [x] Linux - [ ] macOS - [ ] Windows - [ ] Etc. ### Shell - [x] bash - [ ] zsh - [ ] fish ### Problem / Steps to reproduce # Current Behavior With both ```bash $ fzf --prompt=';' ``` and ```bash $ FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS='--prompt=";"' fzf ``` we get the expected output, that the prompt is a semicolon: However, ```bash $ FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS='--prompt=;' fzf # Edited for typo ``` results in what I deduce is shell interpretation of the semicolon, resulting in an empty prompt: # Expected Behavior (What a user might initially/naively expect) ```bash $ FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS='--prompt=;' fzf ``` should produce the same output as `fzf --prompt=';'` and `FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS='--prompt=;' fzf`. # Suggestion I believe I understand why shell interpretation is used: if we execute something like ```bash fzf "$FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS $@" ``` the shell will just treat `$FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS"` as one massive argument to `fzf`. So, something like ```bash bash -c "fzf $FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS $@" ``` is necessary. But it might not be obvious to the average user why their `FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS` string is not working. I was very confused for a while just now, and almost even filed this as bug report haha, when trying to include a custom prompt (`$'--prompt="\033[30;44m\033[0m\033[34m\033[0m "\n'`) in my `FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS`. I searched through `man fzf`, the [official online docs](https://junegunn.github.io/fzf/reference/), and `fzf --bash` to no avail. Perhaps we could have a little note somewhere in the man page warning the user to escape their `FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS` approperiately. That way, if they are stumped like I was, a simple search `/FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS` in `man fzf` will bring this to their attention. This is a skill issue on my part for not realising this earlier, but I think clearer documentation benefits everyone.

title: Limitations of the `result` event for "final result" use cases
author: junegunn
body:
## Problem

The `result` event fires whenever the result list is updated. With a streaming input source, that means it fires many times for a single query as new records arrive and are matched. Today there's no good way to express "do X once when the result for the current query has settled."

## Concrete use cases

- `result:best` (or similar) - user wants to jump to the best match for the new query, not re-jump on every interim snapshot during loading.
- `result:first` / `result:pos(...)` - same shape: a one-shot cursor move after the query is committed.
- Status indicators, conditional actions via `result:transform:...` that depend on the final match count.

## Why current workarounds fall short

- `load` fires once when the input stream closes, but says nothing about subsequent query changes.
- `result` is too noisy during streaming.
- There's no way for a `transform`/`bg-transform` script to check "is more input coming?" or "is this the final snapshot for the current query?"

## Possible directions

### New event (`result-final`?)

Same as `result`, but not triggered when the input stream is still open.

### Expose stream/search status via an environment variable

So that a transform action bound to `result` can conditionally decide what to do.

- `FZF_LOADING` (0/1) - input stream still open.

## Related

- #4825 - `transform-search()+best` chain ordering; depends on a similar "wait for this query's result" primitive.

title: Cache for list of options, for very large directories
author: tbodt
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have read through the manual page (`man fzf`)
- [x] I have searched through the existing issues
- [x] For bug reports, I have checked if the bug is reproducible in the latest version of fzf

### Output of `fzf --version`

0.70.0 (Homebrew)

### OS

- [ ] Linux
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Windows
- [ ] Etc.

### Shell

- [ ] bash
- [ ] zsh
- [x] fish

### Problem / Steps to reproduce

I regularly work in very large directories where even fd or rg takes 5-10 seconds, which is a bad experience to just open a file whose name you know. Realistically though this file list does not change that much, meaning if fzf could remember the list from last time it would only be necessary to wait for new files.

So I'm imagining something like a `--cache` flag that takes a filename. When fzf starts it loads the list from the file, then when items come in from stdin it does the equivalent of "prepend to the list and then remove any duplicates that came in from the cache". And when fzf exits it writes out the full list to the file.

title: multiline PDF search
author: SuperCowProducts
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have read through the manual page (`man fzf`)
- [x] I have searched through the existing issues
- [ ] For bug reports, I have checked if the bug is reproducible in the latest version of fzf

### Output of `fzf --version`

0.73.1 (Fedora)

### OS

- [x] Linux
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Windows
- [ ] Etc.

### Shell

- [x] bash
- [ ] zsh
- [ ] fish

### Problem / Steps to reproduce

https://github.com/junegunn/fzf/issues/1183 helps us search inside a PDF, although multiline fuzzy search is the most useful when searching inside PDFs when looking for a specific subject (which can often be reduced to a combination of single topics). An example of this would be asking the question 'How do I plot a point in SymPy?', and for that I would need to fuzzy search the terms `plot` and `point` over multiple lines (as they would need to come from the same paragraph) inside the SymPy documentation. 

[sympy-docs-pdf-1.14.0.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28914757/sympy-docs-pdf-1.14.0.pdf)

title: CTRL-R no longer working properly in Fish
author: Chippit
body: ### Checklist - [x] I have read through the manual page (`man fzf`) - [x] I have searched through the existing issues - [x] For bug reports, I have checked if the bug is reproducible in the latest version of fzf ### Output of `fzf --version` 0.73.1 (Fedora) ### OS - [x] Linux - [ ] macOS - [ ] Windows - [ ] Etc. ### Shell - [ ] bash - [ ] zsh - [x] fish ### Problem / Steps to reproduce As of a Fedora system update 3 or 4 days ago, the CTRL-R hotkey no longer works as expected in fish. When pressed, I am presented with, instead of the normal list of history commands, a blank list. If I hit ALT-T once here, the expected commands will appear, but hitting enter to select them from here will not paste them into the shell. If I select one of the items and tab to multi-select, what appears to be the right command appears in the right panel, but it also doesn't paste correctly into the shell. Selecting several items will enter several blank lines into the shell. Finally, the number of search items displayed by the count in the CTRL-R menu also doesn't seem to properly align with the number if I use `history | fzf`, which reads `7124/7124` as of writing. CTRL-T, ALT-C and Shift-Tab completion all appear to work as normal, and CTRL-R works okay in Bash also.

title: Override Preview Scroll Index Limit
author: Maple38
body: Currently, if text shown in the preview pane exceeds the height of the window, you're able the scroll down it. The issue is that the scrolling exceeds the actual text contents and will continue until the very last line of text is at the very top of the scroll preview. Now, this makes sense when the preview pane has its dynamic wrapping enabled. But pre-formatted text is passed in and Fzf knows the exact number of lines it will take up, it's a strange limitation. Nothing complicated is needed, just a simple variable for power users would be appreciated — something that overrides the maximum index of scrolling in the preview pane.
